entry.title: New Practice System for Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori (UX100)
entry.content:
Last year, we took the practice system for the Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori (UX100) course offline to upgrade its capabilities to better reflect the latest additions to the course material. This was right before our latest innovation was kicked off internally: SAP Certifications reimagined.

Unfortunately, this shift of priorities led to some delay for this practice system. But now it is here: SAP Fiori – Foundation

  • You get full administrative access to an SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS04 like you would get in the classroom version of the UX100.
  • You get full administrative access to an SAP BTP subaccount with entitlements for SAP Business Application Studio and SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

All exercises can be done without any limitation:

  • Explore a fresh installed SAP S/4HANA with SAP Best Practices in place.
  • Use the fully configured SAP Fiori launchpad.
  • Set up SAP Fiori spaces, pages, and catalogs.
  • Dive into SAP Fiori development and system administration.

It is all here, just one booking away. Are you ready for some exercise(s)?

💡Hint:
UX100 is part of the learning journeys Developing SAPUI5 Applications and SAP Fiori – System Administration, which both lead to a certification. Check-out my blog post summarizing UX100.

entry.title: From Idea to Possibility: Internal Tool Development with SAP BTP, CAP, and Claude Code
entry.content:
Internal teams often understand their own challenges very clearly. Enterprise Architects, IT consultants, business teams, and technical teams all see opportunities where a small internal tool could improve processes, structure information, or support better decisions.

However, turning these ideas into working applications is not always easy. It usually requires knowledge of backend development, frontend structure, databases, deployment, security, and SAP-specific technologies.

This is where SAP BTP, CAP, Cloud Foundry, and SAP Business Application Studio open interesting possibilities.

With these technologies, teams with access to SAP BTP can explore SAP-native internal tools with a structured setup: frontend, backend, services, database persistence, and cloud deployment. Instead of staying only at the idea or slide level, teams can start imagining how their concepts could become real prototypes inside SAP BTP.

Possible internal use cases could include:

  • capability mapping tools
  • architecture decision tracking
  • lightweight reporting dashboards
  • internal knowledge repositories
  • recommendation-based guidance tools
  • structured stakeholder input tools
  • portfolio or transformation tracking tools
  • process support and data collection tools

After deployment, an application can run inside SAP BTP Cloud Foundry and be accessed through the SAP BTP Cockpit:

SAP BTP Cockpit → Cloud Foundry → Spaces → Applications → Selected App → Mapped Route

From there, teams can monitor application status, memory usage, service bindings, instances, and routes. This shows how an idea can move beyond a local prototype and become a cloud-deployed enterprise application.

A useful addition to this setup is Claude Code. It can act as an AI-assisted development helper by supporting CAP project structure, CDS models, service definitions, Node.js handlers, mta.yaml configuration, deployment setup, and debugging.

The main point is not that AI replaces developers. Instead, it can help Enterprise Architects, IT consultants, semi-technical users, and cross-functional teams better understand what is possible, prototype faster, and collaborate more effectively with development teams.

SAP BTP, CAP, Cloud Foundry, BAS, and AI-assisted development show a future where internal tool ideas can move faster from concept to possibility  while still staying aligned with SAP-native technologies

#SAP #SAPBTP #CAP #CloudFoundry #SAPBusinessApplicationStudio #ClaudeCode #AIAssistedDevelopment #EnterpriseArchitecture #ITConsulting #EnterpriseTools #InternalTools #DigitalTransformation

entry.title: Powering unique engagement at Sapphire 2026
entry.content:
Thousands of marketers and CX leaders are heading to Sapphire in Orlando and Madrid this May to join SAP leaders and industry peers for the reveal of a reimagined Joule experience — and our bold new vision for how businesses will run from now on. SAP Engagement Cloud will share exciting innovations and give attendees a front-row seat to how agentic AI is reshaping customer engagement across every channel.

Closing the engagement divide

At Sapphire, you'll hear speakers reference the Engagement Divide — the growing gap between what customers expect and what brands can actually deliver. Research from the 2026 Global Engagement Index Report shows this divide is widening at an unprecedented pace, and AI is compounding it faster than most organizations realize.

The numbers are stark: only 22% of brands recognise they have a problem creating seamless experiences, yet 82% of consumers are dissatisfied with brands. The leading cause? 78% of brands say they cannot manage campaigns with real-time AI optimisation to personalise at scale. This isn't just a marketing problem — it's a fundamental, business-wide challenge.

This year, SAP Engagement Cloud will showcase how B2B and B2C organisations can close this divide by building a foundation that keeps pace with AI and rising consumer demand.

Show floor: a unified customer engine

Head to the demo pods to see the engagement transformation live. The SAP Customer Experience portfolio uses agentic AI to unify customer and operational data, personalise engagement across channels, adapt loyalty programmes in real time, and turn interactions into advocacy.

Orlando - Pod CXP2457 — Marketing and Engagement

Madrid - Pod CXP1461 — Marketing and Engagement

Must-attend sessions

Sapphire Orlando

From campaigns to AI-driven marketing with SAP and Google Cloud

SAP2674 · Tue May 12, 1:30pm · Kelsey Jones, Head of Product Marketing, SAP

Personalisation: data-driven, AI-enhanced engagement that builds loyalty

CXP1499 · Tue May 12, 2:30pm · Kelsey Jones, SAP; Jonathan Martz, CRM Manager, Gibson

[Keynote] Dive in or drift: surfing the agentic customer experience revolution

KEY2518 · Wed May 13, 1:00pm · Balaji Balasubramanian & Jessica Keehn, SAP Customer Experience

Sapphire Madrid

How adidas drives AdiClub member loyalty at scale with SAP Engagement Cloud

CXP1219 · Wed May 20, 2:00pm · Sasan Naservafai & Lorenzo Maggi, SAP

[Keynote] Dive in or drift: surfing the agentic customer experience revolution

KEY1428 · Thu May 21, 11:30am · Balaji Balasubramanian & Jessica Keehn, SAP Customer Experience

Frequently asked questions

I'm not a marketer — are these sessions still relevant for me?

Yes! The suggested sessions are perfect for someone in IT or commerce-related roles as well.

In what sessions will I get to see a demo?

You'll see SAP Engagement Cloud demos at the show floor demo pods: CXP2457 in Orlando and CXP1461 in Madrid, both in the Marketing and Engagement zone. Demos cover unifying customer and operational data with agentic AI to personalise engagement, adapt loyalty programmes in real time, and drive scalable growth.

What product innovations will be showcased at Sapphire?

There will be a reveal of a reimagined Joule experience and our bold new vision for how businesses will run from now on.

I can't attend in person — is any content available virtually?

Yes! Join us May 12–13 at SAP Sapphire Virtual for keynotes, strategy talks, ask the experts, solution deep dives, and road map reviews.

entry.title: Re: Validation Error: Exception Subprocess should start w...
entry.content: Exception subprocess cannot have two error ends. It must have only one end type—either an error end or an end message. Consider moving the router logic inside the LIP

entry.title: Re: New Dates for Get Certified: SAP BTP Administrator Live Sessions
entry.content:
@mts2605 The automatic verification checks your work step by step starting with the directories and subaccounts. If the name or the subdomain is not what is written in the task document, the verification stops with 0%. The reason is quite simple: To check what is actually inside of a subaccount, the verification needs a correct subdomain to access it.

💡Hints:

  • Be accurate in everything you enter.
  • Use copy & paste as often as possible.
  • When copying, check for technical characters like line-breaks.

We are currently tidying up the practice systems. It should hopefully be gone for your S-user in the near future.

entry.title: AI in SAP: Where Value Is Actually Being Created
entry.content:
Everybody has been talking about the massive transformations that we are witnessing in regards AI and enterprise systems. I'm not saying this is not true, I've seen it myself, especially in industries like energy, where operations are becoming more data-driven every day. In many ways, the impact is already there. My question is: where exactly is AI making a difference?

Because even though technology is moving fast, the impact doesn’t always show up at the same pace. In most cases, the problem isn’t whether AI works or not, because it usually does. The real challenge is what happens after the model produces an output, and whether that output actually turns into something the business can act on.

From what we’ve seen in different projects, many organizations are already experimenting with AI across their SAP landscape. Individual use cases are delivering valuable results that go from producing accurate outputs to outperforming traditional approaches. What happens when companies try to take that value beyond a single use case and extend it across processes? Do things start to get complicated?

The challenge usually doesn’t show up when the model is running. It appears a bit later, when teams try to use those outputs in every day operations. Insights might be accurate, but that doesn’t automatically mean they fit into how the business actually works. Maybe, instead of asking ourselves if something works, we should shift the conversation to how to actually use it in a consistent way. 

Fitting a model or an insight into a complex process that already exists is the real challenge. Most companies do not really have issues in generating insights: They can forecast better, process information faster, and detect patterns that were hard to see before. But what comes after? What happens once the insight is there?

In an SAP environment, acting on that insight is rarely a simple step. Even something as straightforward as improving a forecast can have countless effects across different areas. Adjusting inventory levels or procurement volumes doesn’t stay within a single function. It impacts planning, finance, logistics, and sometimes even customer commitments. Making that change actually happen requires aligned data, coordinated processes, and systems that can absorb the adjustment without breaking.

We’ve seen this take place very clearly in our work with a global energy company. The challenge there wasn’t a lack of data or visibility, but how to act on it in a way that was both fast and reliable. Inspecting thousands of kilometers of transmission lines manually was slow, risky, and difficult to scale. By introducing drones combined with AI-based image recognition, they were able to detect potential issues much earlier and speed up decision-making around maintenance.

 

Although the model identifying anomalies made a really big difference, it was how that information connected back into their SAP processes that changed everything. The insights generated in the field needed to trigger actions like maintenance planning and work order creation, without adding more manual steps. Once that connection was in place, the impact became much more tangible: inspections became significantly safer, productivity increased, and response times improved.

This is where a pattern starts to emerge. AI is already delivering value, but mostly in scenarios where the scope is controlled and the dependencies are limited. The challenge appears when organizations try to move beyond those contained use cases and bring AI into core operations.

At that point, the underlying SAP architecture starts to matter a lot more. Data consistency, integration, and process standardization are no longer background topics. They become the difference between an insight that looks good on paper and one that actually drives action. The more companies rely on AI-generated outputs, the more they depend on their systems being able to respond without friction.

What we’ve learned from these experiences is that the shift doesn’t happen when you add more models. It happens when those models become part of how processes run end to end. That’s when AI stops being something experimental and starts becoming part of everyday operations.

So, to answer my initial question: value is not created when a model detects an anomaly or improves a forecast. It’s created when that output leads to a decision that is actually executed. And in SAP environments, that step from insight to action is still where most of the real work is happening today.

AI is already showing what’s possible. But the organizations that will see the biggest impact are the ones that can connect that potential to how their systems actually run.

entry.title: Re: Latest People Profile | Show all fields by default
entry.content:
As per SAP documentation, the behaviour in the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central is expected by design. Currently, there is no configuration or RBP permission available to expand all sections (such as Personal Information, Job Information, Compensation Information, etc.) by default. The profile follows a compressed or collapsed view approach, where users must manually click “Show More” or “View All” to access complete data.

This is confirmed in SAP KBAs such as SAP KBA 3622326, which explains that fields are displayed in a compressed format, and SAP KBA 3593877, which clarifies that this is expected system behaviour and not a defect. Additionally, SAP KBA 3345636 highlights that certain features from the legacy People Profile, such as the ability to expand all sections by default via RBP, are not available in the Latest People Profile.

Therefore, this limitation is consistent across customers and is part of the current product design. Any change to enable default expansion of all fields would require an enhancement request via the SAP Influence Portal.

Hope this answers your query

entry.title: Re: Agentic coding: does it really work, or is it just a skill issue on my side?
entry.content: Hi eamarce23  - its not you, it is the coding agents.  I have been dealing with this for some time now.  I do not know specifically what agents you are using, in which environment, or for what code.  I have found that different models work differently and have different strengths and weakness.  I will not say better or worse but different.  Having said that, for the very reason you mentioned, I have officially "fired" one model entirely.  It was forgetting and just getting things wrong.   For the most part, I have found that the issues are "memory" issues.   I would suggest that you ask the agent what you can do to improve the work.    I have done this with several agents.   One of the agents suggested that a clear design be agreed to up front and is managed in a methodical way.   I guess there is no getting away from that.  It also suggested periodic design versioning, that becomes an "approved" version and the model cannot go back and change it, without permission.    One model got so back that I had to institute a rule that I needed to review every change, but even then it still made mistakes.   Another model, when I asked how to stop these types of issues, suggested that I periodically create "state documents" and then take that document into a new chat and start fresh.  According to the model it has trouble the longer the chat gets.   This seems to help.    Some agents have come back and just said "sorry that's on me" and has moved on.  Does not mean much coming from something that cant actually feel regret.  But at least you know its not you.    I hope this helps.

entry.title: Integration XSUAA protected MCP-Server into custom Joule Agent
entry.content:
Hello there

I have a MCP-Server deployed to Cloud Foundry environment using FastMCP and which is protected using XSUAA from Cloud Foundry (has its own instance). Using the MCP Inspector the authentication and so on works fine.

However now I'm trying to integrate it into Joule Studio using a destination. Configuration::
- Proxy Type: Internet
- URL: <MCP-Server URL> (without /mcp)
- Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange 
- Client ID, etc. from my XSUAA Instance
- Property sap-joule-studio-mcp-server: true

When I'm now trying to add my MCP-Server to my custom Agent in Joule Studio I received the error message:
Unable to find MCP server tools for destination iag-users-mcp-server. No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2UserTokenExchange'.

Also when I'm trying to add the property HTML5.forwardAuthToken: true I received the error message:
Internal server error. Failed to execute HTTP request during MCP server tools listing. Failed to build headers.

It seems that Joule (Studio) is not forwarding the JWT Token from IAS. Does anyone know how to integrate a MCP-Server which is protected by XSUAA?

Thanks and kind regards
Fabian

entry.title: Re: Next Generation Just-In-Time, Supply to Customer - Overview
entry.content:
Can the LZJ scheduling agreement for summarized JIT Calls have a different Ship to business partner than Sold to? The best practices shows same partner number in Sold to and Ship to, and I can make that work. 

But I get "Customer: XXXXX is not a valid JIT Customer if the Sold to and Ship to #s are different in #he header of the LZJ SA.  

In ECC, I don't need to use Unloading Point or Assembly Location in JITV set up. And the JIT Customer is Ship to specific because it's defined by Sold to + Partner Description.  So if there are multiple Ship to Partner Descriptions per Sold to,  by setting up different JIT Customer codes, it works for multiple Ship to's. When creating the LZJ SA, the Sold to and Ship to Partner Description determines which Ship to specific JIT Customer is assigned to that SA.

entry.title: Fiori App for Goods Receipt that Supports GOITEM-ANZGEB (Number of Containers) field?
entry.content:

When posting a Goods Receipt in MIGO, I'm able to use the field GOITEM-ANZGEB ("Number of Containers" — on the Quantity tab). This field is important for our process because we need to capture the container count at the time of GR posting.

 

The problem: our users are moving to Fiori, and I can't seem to find a standard Fiori app that exposes this field during GR posting. I've checked the usual suspects:

 

• Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843 / MIGO\_GR)

 

• My Purchase Order Items - Receive

 

\- Post Goods Movement (Fiori version of MIGO)

Number of Containers value on the item level.

 

My questions:

Any pointers or even confirmation that it's not supported out-of-the-box would be super helpful.

 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Invoice and item numbers on settlement document flexi...
entry.content:
Hi @1214 , 

In your scenario, the best approach is indeed to leverage your split criteria (as you already did) and use the standard fields available in the settlement document:

  • Source Document (WBELNV) → Billing Document Number
  • Source Document Item (POSNRV) → Billing Document Item

These fields can be displayed in Basic Data B at line item level and are also available in the Flexible Overview.

There is no need to split the settlement document per billing document, unless you have a strict business requirement to do so. Using the source document fields is usually sufficient and cleaner.

 

To ensure these fields are populated, you need to configure the Transfer Manager - event 71:

  • Set the Source Document Type (LFTYPV_STD) to “14” (Billing Document)
    This enables the system to correctly populate WBELNV and POSNRV in the settlement document.


To be considered:

Be aware that this approach (especially when splitting at invoice level) can lead to:

  • Performance issues
  • Large number of line items

So it’s important to validate that the level of detail is really required from a business perspective.


Regards, 
Gauthier VICTOR

entry.title: Characteristic Description Blank
entry.content:
Hi SAP gurus 

Your advice and opinions are very welcome. I have spent quite some time with this issue and I have not find how to fix it. I hope you can help me

Problem Description summary:

I made a change to the description of a characteristics using transaction CT04. Now when I look in Specification Workbench (CG02BD) the field for the description is blank

The steps that I followed were:

  • Updated characteristic description in CT04 (Basic Data and Descriptions tab).
  • Verified language-dependent entries in CABN.
  • Used Engineering Change Management (Change Number) where applicable.
  • Confirmed new description was visible in CL02 for the relevant Class Type 100.
  • Executed CGCZ (Match Up Master Data) with the following options:
    • Match up Value Assignment Type and Characteristic
    • Generate Symbols
    • Regenerate Symbol Descriptions
    • Ran CGCZ in the correct logon language.
  • If I use F1 in the blank cell the next screen shows the characteristics with the correct description in full display (example below)

 

From what I have gathered investigating I found that the steps were correct and that what might be happening is "CG02BD is probably being blocked from displaying it because an existing blank characteristic-dependent UDT instance is taking precedence for that specification/value assignment context."

 

There is nothing in the UDT. I have followed some recommendations of changing the status of the spec to allow for a change which was essentially to "delete the blank UDT".  This didnt make too much sense to me but still I tried it with no avail

I made 18 changes in total and now I have 18 x Nr Specifications to fix !!

Thanks very much in advance

Regards

Roberto

entry.title: Inquiry on SAP Build Code Licensing Models and Availability
entry.content:
Hi experts!
 
I hope you are doing well…
 
We would like to extend an inquire about the available licensing models for SAP Build Code.
 
SAP Build Code is an integrated development environment (IDE), similar to SAP Business Application Studio, that includes embedded artificial intelligence capabilities and tools (e.g., Joule for Developers). When reviewing the SAP Pricing App, we were unable to identify a SKU associated with this tool under a subscription-based licensing model. Therefore, we understand that this service may only be available under alternative licensing models (e.g., CPEA or Pay-As-You-Go). We would appreciate your confirmation on this matter, as we are currently working on an initiative with a client who plans to acquire development capabilities through a subscription model; however, when creating the opportunity or building the Bill of Materials, the Build Code service does not appear as an available option under this scheme.
 
Additionally, we would like to confirm the following: if a client wants to develop a Fiori/CAP application using Joule for Developers, is it necessary to do so through SAP Build Code? And if so, would this service only be available under the CPEA model?
 
We look forward to your response and would appreciate any documentation or resources you can share to further clarify this topic.
 
¡Kind regards! 
 
SAP Build Code SAP Business Application Studio Joule

entry.title: (First Post) How to create a "Blanket" TO for RF Scanner use
entry.content:
Hello, 

This is my first post, so I apologize if I have the wrong tags or wrong community linked. If I did do something wrong, please inform me. 

 

We just integrated RF Scanners at our site, using SAP. We are still getting used to them. Currently, when we create a TO, it automatically selects the SU#, and we can't change the exact SU# when picking the TO. Is there a way to create a blanket TO that allows me to pick any SU that matches the requirements? 

For example. I am making a TO to request 5 boxes of "Item 1 Batch A" moved from Room 1 to Room 2. We have 100+ boxes of "Item 1 Batch A" in Room 1, so we want to be able to scan and take the first 5 SU's available for access, rather than dig through all 100 for the exact ones SAP selects. 

Thank you in advance for your assistance. I have spent several hours searching for an answer but have yet to find anything. 

Please let me know if you need any additional information. 

(NOTE: I am an End-User at our site, and do not have the authority in SAP to change the capabilities or settings for any transactions.)

entry.title: SAP DMS Client – A Desktop Companion for Cloud Integration Data Archiving
entry.content:
Why I Built This

While looking into Cloud Integration data archiving, I came across Kishor Gopinathan's blog post SAP Cloud Integration – Data Archiving to BTP SDM. It walks through the full setup — creating service instances, building the CloudIntegration_LogArchive destination, and activating archiving via API.

I followed it while onboarding onto a new Integration Suite landscape, and it worked great. But I noticed that the workflow assumes we have a REST client like Postman at hand to call APIs, and some familiarity with CMIS to browse the archived content. Not everyone on the team has that setup ready — and honestly, assembling JSON payloads and fetching OAuth tokens just to activate archiving felt like unnecessary friction for what should be a straightforward task.

So I built a small desktop app to lower that barrier: the SAP DMS Client. I built it with the help of AI tooling, which made it realistic to go from idea to working desktop app within my onboarding timeline. It reads our DMS and Cloud Integration service keys and gives us a UI for managing repositories, exporting destination files, activating archiving, and browsing archived content — no REST client or CMIS knowledge required. The code is open source, so anyone can inspect and adapt it.

What It Does

The app is built with Electron, React, and Material UI (following SAP Horizon design guidelines). Once we load our two service key files — one for DMS, one for Process Integration Runtime — we get four things:

  • Repository management — create, list, and delete DMS repositories without CMIS API calls
  • Destination export — generate a CloudIntegration_LogArchive.json with all credentials pre-filled, ready to import into BTP Cockpit
  • Archiving activation — activate Cloud Integration data archiving in one click (the app handles the OAuth2 token exchange)
  • File explorer — browse the YYYY/MM/DD folder structure that Cloud Integration creates in the repository

The destination export is probably the biggest time saver. Instead of manually extracting clientId, clientSecret, tokenServiceURL, and the SDM endpoint from the service key JSON, the app assembles the full destination file automatically. We just import it into BTP Cockpit and move on.

Here's what the generated file looks like:

{
  "Name": "CloudIntegration_LogArchive",
  "Type": "HTTP",
  "URL": "https://<sdm-endpoint>/browser",
  "Authentication": "OAuth2ClientCredentials",
  "RepositoryId": "<repository-id>",
  "clientId": "<from-service-key>",
  "clientSecret": "<from-service-key>",
  "tokenServiceURL": "https://<auth-endpoint>/oauth/token"
}

No copy-paste errors, no missed fields.

One thing to note: when we import the destination into BTP Cockpit, all fields are populated automatically except the client secret — BTP doesn't include it in the import format for security reasons. We'll need to grab the clientSecret from our DMS service key file and paste it manually into the destination configuration in the Cockpit after import.

What We Need Before Starting

The same BTP services from the original blog:

Service Plan What We Need

Integration Suiteenterprise / standardCloud Integration capability activated
Process Integration RuntimeapiService instance + service key
Document Management Service, Integration OptionstandardService instance + service key

Setup details: Initial Setup for DMS and Creating Service Key for Cloud Integration.

The app is on GitHub at github.com/sap-ef/da-dms-client. The easiest way to get started is to download a pre-built installer from the Releases page:

  • macOS — .dmg file
  • Windows — .exe installer

Alternatively, we can build from source with Node.js 18+:

git clone https://github.com/sap-ef/da-dms-client.git
cd da-dms-client
npm install
npm run electron:dev

The Workflow in Practice

Once the app is running, the flow follows a specific order — each step depends on the previous one:

  1. Load the DMS service key in Settings — this connects us to our Document Management Service instance
  2. Create a repository — we need a repository before we can generate a destination (the destination includes the repository ID)
  3. Export the destination file — the app generates the CloudIntegration_LogArchive.json with all the values from the service key
  4. Import the destination into the Integration Suite subaccount — in BTP Cockpit under Connectivity > Destinations (remember to fill in the client secret manually after import)
  5. Load the Cloud Integration service key in Settings — this connects us to the Process Integration Runtime instance
  6. Activate data archiving — this only works if the destination already exists in BTP, otherwise Cloud Integration won't know where to write the logs

 

Don't expect logs to appear immediately. As described in the original blog, the log collection process runs on a 7-day cycle — so it may take several days before the first archived files show up in the repository.

After archiving is active, the File Explorer lets us browse what Cloud Integration has stored. The folder structure is date-based (2026/04/15/) with .zip archives of message processing logs for each day.

Conclusion

Kishor Gopinathan's original blog remains the foundation for understanding Cloud Integration data archiving to SDM. The DMS Client just makes the repetitive parts easier so we can spend less time on plumbing and more on the integration itself.

The project is open source at github.com/sap-ef/da-dms-client. If you've dealt with Cloud Integration archiving in your landscape, I'd be curious to hear how you've approached it — or if there are other parts of the DMS workflow that could use better tooling. Drop a comment or open an issue.

Links

  • SAP DMS Client — GitHub
  • SAP Cloud Integration – Data Archiving to BTP SDM — Kishor Gopinathan
  • Initial Setup for Document Management Service
  • Creating Service Instance and Service Key (Cloud Integration)

entry.title: Workflow Processor Not Displayed for Sales Order
entry.content:
We have identified a situation where the workflow processor is not displayed in the approval workflow for a Sales Order Without Charge in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Observed behavior
The approval workflow is triggered, but the Processors section is blank
The message “No users found for task” appears in approval details
The workflow is cancelled automatically
The sales order is not visible in Manage Sales Orders apps, but can still be accessed via display transactions (e.g., VA03 equivalent)


Context
Multiple workflows are active for sales orders
One workflow has no start condition (generic)
Another workflow has specific conditions (e.g., approval reason for Free of Charge orders)


Question
Why does the system trigger the incorrect workflow scenario?
How does SAP determine which workflow is executed when multiple workflows are active?
What is the correct way to avoid this issue?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Workflow Processor Not Displayed for Sales Order
entry.content:
This behavior is caused by overlapping active workflow variants in Flexible Workflow for Sales Orders.

Root Cause

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the system evaluates all active and valid workflow variants for the same business object (SalesOrder).
The workflow engine will trigger the first variant whose start conditions evaluate as true.

If a workflow is configured without start conditions, it becomes a generic workflow and will match all sales orders, including those intended for more specific workflows (such as Sales Orders Without Charge).

As a result:

  • The generic workflow is triggered first
  • If no valid agent is determined → “No users found for task”
  • The workflow is cancelled
  • The document remains unreleased and may not appear in certain Fiori apps

Why the Correct Scenario Is Not Triggered

Even if a dedicated workflow exists (e.g., for Free of Charge orders), it will not execute if:

  • Another active workflow already matches first
  • Its start conditions are broader or undefined

There is no explicit priority setting between workflows — execution depends entirely on:

  • Active status
  • Validity period
  • Start condition evaluation

Resolution

To prevent this issue:

  1. Restrict or deactivate generic workflows
    • Add start conditions to ensure they only apply to relevant scenarios
    • OR deactivate them if not required
  2. Ensure specific workflows are clearly defined
    • Example: Approval reason, document type, or other filters
  3. Avoid overlapping conditions
    Make workflow conditions mutually exclusive where possible

Validation

After adjustment:

  • Create a new Sales Order Without Charge
  • Check Manage Workflow Instances to confirm the correct workflow scenario is triggered
  • Review logs via Application Log (object: SLS_SO_APPROVAL_WF) if needed

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud: “Enter value in permitted format” error in Pricing Elements – Flexible Analysis
entry.content:
Hi,

When trying to run the “Pricing Elements – Flexible Analysis” app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (public edition), we receive the following error:

For characteristic 2CLXSC0SW771A5B1VRWY89H9WG9, enter value in a permitted format

This issue was observed in multiple systems (e.g. DEV and TEST). In some cases, the app also shows no data.

We verified that:

  • The underlying CDS query is available
  • The app loads but fails upon execution

Has anyone encountered this issue before or can advise on how to troubleshoot it?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud: “Enter value in permitted format” erro...
entry.content:
Yes, this issue can occur due to invalid user personalization (saved variant / cached filter values) rather than a backend or CDS problem.

Root Cause

The characteristic mentioned in the error corresponds to a CDS parameter (in this case:
“Number of Evaluation Days” – P_NUMBEROFEVALUATIONDAYS), which expects a numeric (integer) value.

If a user has previously saved:

  • an invalid value (e.g. text, blank, incorrect format), or
  • a corrupted variant / personalization

then the app attempts to reuse that value during startup, which triggers the error:

“Enter value in a permitted format”

 

Solution

  1. Open the app and manually enter a valid numeric value (e.g. 30) for the affected field
  2. Execute the app once
  3. Exit and reopen the app

This refreshes the personalization cache and resolves the issue.

Alternative (if issue persists)

You can also reset personalization:

  • In the app:
    Manage Variants → Reset / Delete Custom Variant
  • Or via Launchpad:
    User Settings → App Personalization → Reset

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Impossible to select the value of ALV dropdown if only one value
entry.content:
The bug is probably solved by this SAP note, whose fix comes with SAP GUI for Windows 800 patch level 5:

3365595 - SAP GUI for Windows ALV Grid control: The value of the dropdown cell disappears when you enter the values via keyboard and hit ENTER. - SAP for Me

There's a list of themes which are buggy, like Quartz Dark and Belize.

Some themes are fine, like Blue Crystal.

As far as I can see, the issue is only when there's only one value in the dropdown listbox.

If you can't install the latest SAP GUI patch, there are 2 workarounds:

  1. If you want to keep the SAP GUI theme Quartz Dark:
    1. Type manually T (which selects the TYPE value):
    2. Press the dropdown button once:
    3. Press the dropdown button again (total = twice):
  2. You may change the SAP GUI theme, for instance Blue Crystal, the dropdown list is shown correctly:

For information, the issue is that the height of the dropdown listbox is incorrectly calculated globally for some themes and specifically the height zero if there's only one value, as can be seen via the demo program BCALV_TEST_GRID, here with the buggy theme Belize:

With my demo data, TIME has 1 value (and shows nothing at all) and COUNTRYFR shows 2 values (scroll to see the second value):

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Fine-Grained Access Control in SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
entry.content:
Introduction

When working with SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) across multiple brokers, it is essential to enforce strict user access controls. This ensures adherence to the separation of concerns principle and protects provisioned brokers from unauthorized actions. Without appropriate controls, users may inadvertently disrupt services—such as by deleting a broker or spinning up new brokers—which can compromise the environment or affect other users.

While AEM offers standard inbuilt roles designed for granular access management, these roles often do not align with the specific combinations required for specific landscape setup. For example, you may want users to have the ability to manage queues (create, edit, delete) within a specific broker, but restrict their ability to perform any other critical actions elsewhere. Achieving this level of intermediate access control is possible with Group management feature of AEM in conjunction with external Identity Provider (IdP) configurations.

To learn how to set up the Cloud Identity Service, refer to the blog post: “Streamlining User Management in Advanced Event Mesh with SAP CIS and Identity Federation.”

This blog post, co-authored by Sunny Kapoor and Tobias Griebe outlines the necessary steps to set up and enforce such granular access, ensuring users have only the permissions needed for their designated tasks—thereby protecting your AEM environment from accidental disruptions.

Why User Groups over Users in User Management of AEM?

While AEM offers standard inbuilt roles, these typically grant either full access to a feature set or restrict users to viewer-only permissions. In scenarios where controlled, intermediate access is required—such as delegating limited management of a specific event broker—standard roles may not suffice. This is where the “Mission Control User” role becomes essential and is available only if you have user groups configured. Mission Control Users have limited viewing access in Cluster Manager. They can't see event broker services they are not assigned permissions to, and they have no access to Mesh Manager. Users can be given greater access to event broker services by assigning permissions to them. For more information about event broker service-specific access levels, see Configuring User Access to Event Broker Services.

Prerequisites

Before you enable group and role mapping for your advanced event mesh account, you need to do the following:Configure your SAP Cloud Identity Service instance to provide claim values that you can map to user groups. For more information, see the SAP Cloud Identity Services documentation.

Also, you can refer to the blog post: “Streamlining User Management in Advanced Event Mesh with SAP CIS and Identity Federation.”

Creating a User Group

In this example configuration, we have created two User Groups by following the official documentation.First user group called Administrators where we have assigned two roles – Account Administrator and Insights Advanced Editor.

Second User Group called Participants where we have assigned single role i.e. Mission Control Users that can access Cluster Manager but have limited access and viewing capabilities.

Further event broker service access levels i.e. Viewer, Editor or Manager needs to be granted to user groups with the Mission Control User as per the requirement. In this example, we will assign Editor user access to one of the event brokers so that users with Participants group assignment can manage queues (create, edit, delete) within a specific broker, but restrict their ability to perform any other critical actions.

Group Management

You can assign roles to user groups rather than directly to individual users. By mapping claims received from your identity provider (IdP) to these user groups, users can be automatically assigned to the appropriate groups upon authentication. When group management is enabled, users are automatically added to groups based on the claim mapping that you have configured. For example, if your IdP returns a claim values such as “service_manager”, users with that claim value would be automatically assigned to the user group that the claim value is mapped to. If no claim returned for the user is mapped to a group when a user authenticates, the user can be added to a default role or denied access.

After you configure group management you can continue to invite new users manually or you can enable just-in-time provisioning to add new users to groups based on the existing claim mapping configuration when a user successfully authenticates using SSO.

To set up group management for your account, see Configuring Group Management.

In this example we do click on the “Group Management” button in the user groups screen and do the configuration as per the screenshot to enable it. It mainly have the mapping of claims received from your IdP to the user groups created earlier.

  1. This is the attribute key sent from the IdP to AEM.
  2. Groups are configured in the IdP, and users are added to these groups within the IdP.

Configure user access in the Cluster Manager

Last step is to choose the specific event broker for which you like to further control the access like in this example we will assign Editor user access to one of the event brokers so that users with Participants group assignment can manage queues (create, edit, delete) within a specific broker, but restrict their ability to perform any other critical actions.

In Cluster Manager, select the Event Broker, click the three dots, and choose Set User Access.

Click Add User Groups, select the Participants group, and set access to Editor and finally Save.

Important Points to Consider

  1. Advanced event mesh also lets you enable just-in-time provisioning, which lets you onboard new advanced event mesh users without inviting them manually. If you don't select this option, you'll need to manually invite each user in AEM. Note that the Invite button in the Users tab is disabled when group management is enabled, so you'll have to disable group management every time you want to invite users.
  2. Do not select Customize Default User Group and select a user group from the Default User Group drop-down list that has the least privileges without proper testing. When selected, if no defined claim mappings match the user claim value, the user is added to the specified group. This could unintentionally lower privileges for all users, including administrators, which might make it impossible to change user management settings and could lead to the tenant becoming unusable. When not selected, if no claim value for the user is mapped to a user group, the user is denied access to advanced event mesh.
  3. Make sure you test the setup by Clicking Test Access > Run Test to test the updates to ensure that your Account Administrator account is allowed access with the new settings. A message confirms whether you will still have Account Administrator privileges on the account after the settings are applied. If you will lose Account Administrator access, update the settings before continuing.

Conclusion

With this configuration, when a non-admin user logs in, they will only have visibility of the specific event broker to which access has been granted. They will be able to manage queues (create, edit, delete) within that broker, while their ability to perform other critical actions—such as creating or deleting an event broker—will remain restricted.

entry.title: Re: Cannot post intercompany billing due to billing date
entry.content:
Dear @gkodo,

The reason is the billing/accounting posting date, not the STO itself. In this case the billing/accounting posting date is determined as 01.05.2026, while the current date is 29.04.2026. Therefore, when the billing document is created the system tries to post the related accounting document with a future date. In Public Cloud billing documents are normally posted automatically to Financial Accounting when they are created. SAP also provides the Flexible Billing Document Posting option with BLOCK_AUTO_POSTING, which can be used to control or block automatic FI posting of future-dated billing documents.

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3547217

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/e564b50ebc0e45d2ad8719260da3f9b4.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Dynamic Field Behavior Using Side Effects
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP RAP applications, handling dynamic field behavior is a common requirement, especially when UI properties like readonly or mandatory need to change based on user input. Developers often rely on side effects to react to such changes. However, a subtle yet important gap exists,while side effects refresh field values, they do not automatically trigger feature control that governs field behavior. This can lead to confusion when expected UI changes do not occur. In this blog, we will explore this common issue and understand the correct approach to ensure that field behavior is updated dynamically using side effects and permissions.

For example, if the Status can have some data, the Description field should become mandatory and if status doesnot have any data we have to make Description field become read only.

field ( features : instance ) Description;

side effects {
field Status affects field Description;
}

However, this approach only refreshes the field value and does not impact its behavior. As a result, the feature control logic,such as setting a field to readonly, mandatory, or hidden is not triggered. This means that even though the dependent field is refreshed on the UI, its properties remain unchanged, leading to inconsistencies between the expected and actual behavior. Developers may assume that side effects automatically handle both value and behavior updates, but in reality, they only ensure data refresh. To properly influence UI behavior, an additional step is required to explicitly trigger the feature control mechanism.

Solution Overview

  • To ensure that the field behavior is updated correctly, the side effects must explicitly refresh the field permissions instead of just the field value. By doing so, the framework is instructed to re-evaluate the feature control logic, which in turn triggers the feature instance responsible for handling properties like readonly, mandatory, or visibility. This approach ensures that any changes in the dependent field are accurately reflected not only in the data but also in the UI behavior, resulting in a consistent and expected user experience.
  • Whenever Status changes, RAP will re-trigger the feature control logic for the Description field. This ensures that any conditions defined in the feature implementation (for example, making Description readonly when Status = Rejected) are applied immediately on the UI.
side effects {
field Status affects permissions ( field Description );
}

By using permissions, the feature instance is triggered, and the field behavior (such as readonly or mandatory) is updated dynamically as expected.

Interface Behavior Definition

managed implementation in class ZBP_R_TRAV_DBTT unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;
extensible;
define behavior for ZR_TRAV_DBTT alias ZrTravDbtt
persistent table ZTRAV_DBTT
extensible
draft table ZTRAV_DBTT_D
etag master LocalLastChangedAt
lock master total etag LastChangedAt
authorization master( global )
{
  field ( mandatory : create )
   TravelID;

  field ( readonly )
   LocalCreatedBy,
   LocalCreatedAt,
   LocalLastChangedBy,
   LocalLastChangedAt,
   LastChangedAt;

  field ( readonly : update )
   TravelID;

   field(features : instance) Description;
   side effects { field Status affects permissions ( field Description);}


  create;
  update;
  delete;

  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;

  mapping for ZTRAV_DBTT corresponding extensible
  {
    TravelID = TRAVEL_ID;
    Description = DESCRIPTION;
    Status = STATUS;
    LocalCreatedBy = LOCAL_CREATED_BY;
    LocalCreatedAt = LOCAL_CREATED_AT;
    LocalLastChangedBy = LOCAL_LAST_CHANGED_BY;
    LocalLastChangedAt = LOCAL_LAST_CHANGED_AT;
    LastChangedAt = LAST_CHANGED_AT;
  }

}

Instance Features Implementation

method get_instance_features.

  read entities of zr_trav_dbtt in local mode entity zr_trav_dbtt
    all fields with corresponding #( keys ) result data(lt_travel).

  result = value #( for entity in lt_travel
                    ( %tky = entity-%tky
                      %field-description = cond #( when entity-status is not initial
                                                   then if_abap_behv=>fc-f-mandatory
                                                   else if_abap_behv=>fc-f-read_only ) ) ).


endmethod.


Explanation

  • Reads current entity data (ZR_TRAV_DBTT) using provided keys into LT_TRAVEL
  • Uses IN LOCAL MODE to consider unsaved (buffered) changes
  • Loops through each record to determine field behavior dynamically
  • Maps %TKY to link feature control to the correct instance
  • Applies conditional logic on Status field
  • If Status is filled then Description becomes Mandatory
  • If Status is empty then Description becomes Read-only
  • Populates RESULT to control UI behavior at runtime
  • Works with side effects (affects permissions) to re-trigger feature logic on field change

Preview:

If the status field contains a value, then the description field becomes mandatory.

 

 

If the status field has no value, then the description field is read-only.

 

Realtime Scenario

  • RAP behavior definition to dynamically disable the Approval Status field when the Service Entry Sheet item is already approved. This ensures that once the item reaches a “valid” approved state, the field becomes read-only, preventing any further modifications and maintaining data integrity.
  • You used side effects to automatically trigger a UI refresh whenever the approval status changes. This ensures that as soon as the item is approved, the UI immediately reflects the updated state by disabling the field without requiring a manual refresh, providing a seamless and responsive user experience.

Conclusion

When working with dynamic field behavior in RAP, it is important to understand that standard side effects only refresh field values and do not trigger feature control. To ensure that properties like readonly or mandatory are updated correctly, side effects must be defined using permissions. This approach guarantees that the feature instance is executed and the UI behavior responds accurately to changes in dependent fields.

Resources

SAP HELP DOCUMENTATION: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/side-effects

entry.title: Re: How Actual and planning data cab be reported combined...
entry.content:
@phaniadapa 

Option 1 and 2 are some of the common approaches followed. 

1. Can we store actuals also in SAC along with planning? Is it a good approach? : Yes you can store Actuals in SAC. You would need to establish an import connection with Datasphere and load the data. Now whether this is a good approach or no depends on what do you use your actuals for. Do Actuals play any role in your plan like seeding the plan with Actuals etc. If Actuals play a role this approach is well suited. Also how much is the data volume for Actuals. You could always summarize the Actuals to the granularity of your plan data while loading. Since both Actuals and Plan reside in the same model, reporting is simpler in SAC.

2. Should I access actual data from data sphere to SAC using live connection and prepare the plan data in SAC. In this case, is plan data stores in SAC or shall I push the plan data also to Data sphere to do the combine reporting from Data sphere to SAC using live connection. This is also a possible approach well suited when Actuals only play a reporting role. Combining Actuals with live connection with local SAC plan data in SAC layer has its own share of challenges. It also depends on the kind of SAC and Datasphere formation you have. The newer formations offer consumption of live Datasphere models within SAC models in a read only setup which is well suited for your case.

You can also use Datasphere to combine Actuals and Plan and then serve your reporting from Datasphere Analytic Models.

Below are some links which will will help you evaluate the option.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/43509d67b8b84e66a30851e832f66911/fe958749e31e4130874713cb722cd255.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/09442291da174b48a2278b4f63ecfadc.html#task_sx3_rjp_1hc

https://me.sap.com/notes/2832606

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: CA03 Transaction Code Question
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

On CA03 transaction, I need to get the “Allocation” table records in a custom ABAP report.

We identified that the standard function module ‘CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2’ can be used to get the flattened BOM. But we are struggling to match this flat BOM with the allocations table PLMZ.

Since we use phantom materials on SAP, a single material can have multiple components, and each component can have many sub components and so on. This can result in having BOMs with many depth levels.

The problem is that when the same component appears twice at the same depth level, the flat BOM from standard function module ‘CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2’ cannot be perfectly joined with PLMZ table, because a key value is missing. This key value is “Path in exploded BOM tree”.

On PLMZ table, we have field KANTE (Path in exploded BOM tree), and on function module ‘CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2’, we get field WEGXX (Path Predecessor). Both fields seem to have the same purpose: to differentiate when a component appears twice at the same depth level. But the problem is that both fields have different values. Since both values are different, the join condition doesn’t match and shows no data.

entry.title: Re: Romanian DSPL 394 – XML Errors
entry.content:
Hello!

Based on the official ANAF documentation :

- Operations A / AI are allowed only for tip_partener = 1

- NOT allowed for tip_partener = 2

 

Important Business Clarification (from a legal and ANAF perspective):

- RO + number (e.g., RO13279412) → VAT-registered entity

- Number without RO → Non-VAT registered entity

 

The key point is SAP does NOT rely only on the “RO” prefix in the VAT number

Instead, SAP determines the partner type based on a combination of master data attributes, such as:

- VAT registration status

- Tax category used

- Internal classification logic

Even if the VAT number includes “RO”, SAP may still treat the BP incorrectly if the underlying setup is incomplete.

 

To ensure SAP correctly assigns “tip_partener = 1”, all the following must be properly maintained:

- VAT number must be Correctly formatted: RO13279412

- VAT Registration Indicator. BP must be explicitly marked as  VAT-registered taxpayer

 

The issue is not the VAT number itself, but how SAP interprets the Business Partner. Once SAP correctly identifies the partner as a VAT-registered legal entity, the XML will be generated correctly and accepted by ANAF.

The VAT registration number/CUI/Other registration number must be defined on the Business Partner Classification (RO) tab when maintaining master data for your Business Partners in the Manage Business Partner Master Data app.

For SAP to correctly classify a Business Partner as a VAT‑registered legal entity (tip_partener = 1), the VAT number must be maintained under the specific tax category “RO0 – VAT Registration Number.”  If the VAT number is stored under a different category (such as RO1), SAP does not recognize it as a VAT registration, even if it includes the “RO” prefix. As a result, the system incorrectly classifies the partner as a natural person (tip_partener = 2), which leads to the D394 validation errors.

entry.title: Re: Project with Revenue with don´t see to edit Settlemen...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

I hope you are well. 

Please refer to What's New document Profitability Segment Assignment for Projects with Revenue (page 772) which describes this change in behavior in 2602.

With this feature, for projects with revenue, you can now assign a profitability segment to the project or to a WBS element in the hierarchy, if either of them has been marked as a billing element.
To add the profitability segment, use the Assign link available for the new Profitability Segment field that has been added to the Control tab.

With this new feature, the Settlement Rule button will be disabled and unavailable for revenue projects.

3734905- 'Settlement Rule' Button Is Missing In 'Project Control - Enterprise Projects' App For Project Profile 'Project With Revenue'
 https://me.sap.com/notes/3734905

Best regards, 

Tiago 
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: First Half 2026 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors HCM?
entry.content:
Today’s business environment is complex and uncertain, but one thing is clear—when your people and your business move in sync, you can respond to change with clarity and confidence.

With the SAP SuccessFactors HCM First Half 2026 Release, we are delivering more than 400 innovations across all solution areas, enabling you to strengthen connections across your people, data, and processes. Here’s a snapshot of what you can expect to see in this release:

Manage your workforce with agility

AI agents across core HR and payroll
We are introducing new agents designed to simplify tasks and enhance data accuracy:

  • Pending Workflow Agent – Surfaces workflow status and keeps tasks on track by nudging approvers or rerouting to avoid bottlenecks. It is designed to help cut time-to-resolution by up to 40%.
  • Employee Data Integration Agent – Detects data inconsistencies across core HR and payroll, proposes fixes, and applies approved changes to keep records accurate.
  • Payroll Alert Resolution Agent – Analyzes alerts, pinpoints likely root causes, and recommends prioritized fixes. It also enables natural‑language creation of validation rules.

Employee Data Integration Agent

 

Latest home page innovations
Web users can now view cards in the “Needs Attention” section as a full page as well as expand all custom cards into a single view, making it easier to scan, filter, and interact with many cards at once. Additionally, custom background images are now supported in the greeting section, allowing customers to adjust the look and feel to their preference.

Latest home page

 

Core HR workflow notification enhancements
Enhanced workflow notifications are now available, supporting faster, better decisions. Customers can use field mapping to define variables for these notifications to include pending (to-be-approved) data, providing approvers with the key context they need directly in email.

Enhanced demand definition in SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Scheduling
Organizations can now define labor needs by work center and shift, specifying headcount, required skills, and utilization. This provides a clear, consolidated view of planned resources while allowing planners to easily adjust and customize requirements.

Develop your people with purpose

AI agents across learning and talent management
We are introducing new agents designed to help optimize talent development processes:

  • Goal Creation Agent – Helps employees generate meaningful, actionable, and relevant goals aligned to their role and business priorities, delivering clarity in minutes.
  • Goal Progress Agent – Delivers continuous quality assurance by automatically spotting outdated, misaligned, or at‑risk goals and prompting timely refinements.
  • Learning Compliance Agent – Monitors training statuses and prompts employees to complete required learning on time, providing step-by-step guidance to keep users on track.
  • Succession Planning Agent – Provides proactive insights into succession plan health, surfaces risks, and delivers actionable insights to strengthen succession pipelines.

Succession Planning Agent

 

Enriched job architecture
The job profile builder now supports multilevel structures, allowing organizations to mirror real-world complexity. Skills and attributes cascade automatically across levels to streamline maintenance and provide clearer career pathways.

SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors integration
A new productized integration between SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, and SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding helps ensure data consistency and a seamless experience. Hiring teams benefit from SSO and a unified look and feel, simplifying interactions and enabling a faster time to hire.

SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors

 

Learning with intelligent Q&A
Users can now use Joule to surface immediate answers to questions about content in SAP SuccessFactors Learning. Drawing from courses, videos, documents, and other published materials, Joule delivers relevant resources and reference links to help learners find the information they need while reducing time spent navigating multiple sources.

Intelligent Q&A

 

Adapt to change with insight

New intelligent content
Customers can now access new intelligent content for People Intelligence in SAP Business Data Cloud, helping support more informed people and business decisions. With pay transparency insights, organizations can strengthen compliance and support fair pay practices by analyzing compensation and identifying pay gaps. This capability provides the visibility needed to help meet the EU Pay Transparency Directive requirements. We are also introducing intelligent content for onboarding insights, time management insights, benefits insights, rewards and recognition insights, and compensation planning insights.

Pay Transparency Insights

 

Extend applications with ease

Work tech innovations
We are enhancing the SAP SuccessFactors app for Microsoft Teams to make it easier for employees to complete HR tasks directly within the flow of work. In this release, we are introducing calendar sync notifications for absence changes, helping ensure schedules stay up to date when an absence is updated or cancelled. Additionally, the Clock My Time quick action now supports geofencing so that employees can use Microsoft Teams on a supported mobile device to record time when physically present in an approved location. Finally, the Microsoft Teams app now supports onboarding notifications and HR ticket creation and tracking.

Geofencing in Microsoft Teams

 

Extensibility wizard
A new embedded extensibility wizard from SAP Business Technology Platform brings a guided, in-app experience to create custom extensions in SAP SuccessFactors HCM—whether adding a new data field, designing a UI card, or orchestrating a business process. This enables teams to accelerate delivery, align HR workflows to unique requirements, and streamline operations without compromising governance.

Extensibility Wizard

 

Learn more

These are just a few of the many innovations in the SAP SuccessFactors First Half 2026 Release. To learn more, check out the resources below:

  • Visit the SAP SuccessFactors Product Release & Road Map Information page for release documentation, product videos, and solution area blogs
  • Watch the 1H 2026 Release Highlights Overview Video, featuring Bianka Woelke, GVP Head of Application Product Management
  • Discover all innovations in the What's New Viewer

entry.title: France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content:
What Is SIREN — and Why Does It Matter for France E-Invoicing and E-Reporting?

SIREN (Système d'Identification du Répertoire des ENtreprises) is France's unique identification number for a company. This 9-digit number identifies legal entities in France and serves as your electronic address for exchanging e-invoices with your business partners as well as for submitting e-reports to the French authority under the new e-invoicing and e-reporting mandate.

A few important remarks:

  • In the test system, you can only use a test SIREN number — not your real company's SIREN number.
  • In production system, there are multiple ways to register your electronic address, including using only SIREN, SIREN_Suffix, SIREN_SIRET, or SIREN_SIRET_Routing Code. In test or qualification system, using only SIREN as the participant ID would be the only option for now.

 

How to Request SIREN Numbers for Testing?

The official step-by-step guidance is published (in French) by authority on the Chorus Pro portal:

Reference: Utiliser l'environnement de Qualification du Portail de Services

To summarize, the process works as follows:

  1. Register an account: On https://qualif.chorus-pro.gouv.fr, after registering with your email address, you will receive a confirmation email within a few minutes. Click the link in that email to create your password.
  2. Log in with your email and password: Once registration and password setup is complete.
  3. Requesting a Data mattress: After logging in, navigate to the tile Consult the data mattress (Consultation du matelas de données) as demonstrated below. Another important remark is that you need to select the standard data mattress for enterprises (not the ones for Approved Platform which is a different category).

Generate a test data mattress

 4. Find the test SIREN number: After the csv file is generated, download it and find the test SIREN numbers. What you are looking for are the 4 test SIREN numbers that belong to private entity (as highlighted below).

Test SIREN numbers for private enterprises

 Now that we get the test SIREN numbers, let's pick one of them 470221990 and use it to register a participant ID in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

 

Create a Participant ID for testing and activate it to receive electronic document in the Peppol network

For the detailed step-by-step instructions, refer to the below SAP Help documentation:

  • SAP Help - Creating a Participant for Your Company
  • SAP Help - Registering Your Company to Receive Electronic Documents in the Peppol Network

To test the France B2B e-invoicing and e-reporting mandate, you need to register participant using the Party ID type: 0225. The process is quite straightforward:

 

After registration is done, you could also check your test participant ID is registered in the qualification system of French public directory via this link: https://qas.facturation.chorus-pro.gouv.fr/annuaire/

 The below link is to check your real SIREN in production system of French public directory: https://facturation.chorus-pro.gouv.fr/annuaire/.

 

Recommendation: Register Two SIRENs — One for Sending, One for Receiving

To test the full end-to-end flow — both outgoing invoice transmission and incoming invoice reception — you could register two participant IDs with two SIRENs. The recommended setup is:

  • Company Code 1 (Sender): Configured with SIREN A. This company code will send e-invoices to Company Code 2.
    • Maintain SIREN A in Company Code 1 as described in Maintaining VAT Registration Number for Company Code
    • Maintain SIREN B in the Customer Master Data as the buyer's electronic address as described in Maintaining VAT Registration Number for Business Partners
  • Company Code 2 (Receiver): Configured with SIREN B. This company code will receive e-invoices.
    • Maintain SIREN A in Company Code 1 as described in Maintaining VAT Registration Number for Company Code

 With this setup, Company Code 1 sends an invoice addressed to SIREN B, and Company Code 2 receives it — allowing you to validate both the outgoing and incoming flows in a single test cycle. The electronic invoices will appear in your Manage Electronic Documents app or eDocument Cockpit in your SAP system.

Useful Resources:

  • Utiliser l'environnement de Qualification du Portail de Services
  • SAP Help - Creating a Participant for Your Company
  • SAP Help - Registering Your Company to Receive Electronic Documents in the Peppol Network

entry.title: EWM - Choosing Pick Level bins first for stock removal
entry.content:
Hello,

I am looking for advice on how to optimize our picking process in SAP EWM. We want the system to fully deplete our preferred pick level before looking at overstock bins, specifically when dealing with batches that have identical expiration dates.

Current State & Landscape:

  • We have a primary Pick Face (Section 1010 / WLK) and an Overstock/Reserve area (Section 1020 / FKL).

  • The vast majority of our stock is Batch Managed.

  • Multiple pallets/batches often share the exact same Shelf Life Expiration Date (FEFO).

The Problem: When there is a tie in the expiration date, SAP does not prioritize the 1010 Pick Face. Instead, it directs pickers to the 1020 Overstock area. It appears the system is defaulting to the put-away date or picking in an alphanumeric bin order as a tie-breaker, which creates inefficient walking paths for the pickers.

What we have configured and tested so far:

  • Stock Removal Rule: We adjusted our rule (YNPF) to solely rely on the Shelf Life Expiration Date  in Ascending order. We explicitly removed the Put-away Date  to prevent it from being the tie-breaker.

My Question: If multiple batches have the exact same, how can we force SAP EWM to respect the Storage Type Search Sequence (exhausting 1010 before moving to 1020) instead of falling back to an alphanumeric bin sort or hidden put-away date logic?

Is there an additional strict sequence setting, a specific Stock Removal Control flag,  required to enforce this location hierarchy during a FEFO tie?

Thank you in advance for your insights!

entry.title: TIMESTAMP Date Filtering Issue in Signavio Process Intelligence (Epoch Conversion)
entry.content:
Issue Description:
We are attempting to apply a SQL filter in Signavio Process Intelligence to extract records within a specific date range when the field is of type TIMESTAMP.

The source timestamp is stored in ISO 8601 format, for example:
2019-02-23T19:51:57.132+00:00.

Our goal is to extract records between January 1, 2026 and December 31, 2026. However, when previewing an extracted record in the Data View Preview, the timestamp appears as an epoch value (e.g., 1550949650983).

Could you please advise on the correct way to handle this timestamp conversion and apply an accurate date‑range SQL filter for data extraction in Signavio Process Intelligence?
